member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.Categories
member.summary: Defines diagnostic categories used by FluentUnions analyzers to classify the types of issues they detect.
member.remarks: These categories help developers and tools (like IDE filters) organize and prioritize diagnostics. Each category represents a specific aspect of code quality that the analyzers evaluate.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.Categories.Usage
member.summary: Category for diagnostics related to incorrect or suboptimal usage of FluentUnions types.
member.remarks: This category includes issues such as accessing values without proper checks, comparing Option types with null, or using patterns that could lead to runtime errors.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.Categories.Design
member.summary: Category for diagnostics related to design patterns and best practices with FluentUnions.
member.remarks: This category includes recommendations for using more idiomatic patterns, such as preferring Match over if-else chains or using None instead of default constructors.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.Categories.Performance
member.summary: Category for diagnostics related to performance considerations when using FluentUnions.
member.remarks: This category includes issues that might impact runtime performance, such as unnecessary allocations or inefficient patterns.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds
member.summary: Defines unique diagnostic IDs for all FluentUnions analyzers.
member.remarks: Diagnostic IDs follow a consistent pattern: - FU0001-FU0099: Option-related diagnostics - FU0101-FU0199: Result-related diagnostics Each ID uniquely identifies a specific diagnostic rule that can be suppressed or configured independently.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.OptionValueAccessWithoutCheck
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting unsafe access to Option.Value without checking if the option has a value.
member.remarks: This diagnostic warns when code accesses the Value property of an Option without first verifying that IsSome is true, which could lead to an InvalidOperationException at runtime.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.OptionPreferMatchOverIfElse
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for suggesting the use of Match method instead of if-else chains with Option types.
member.remarks: This diagnostic promotes more functional and idiomatic code by suggesting Match when developers use if (option.IsSome) else patterns.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.OptionNullComparison
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting null comparisons with Option types.
member.remarks: Since Option is a value type (struct), it can never be null. This diagnostic warns when code compares an Option with null and suggests using IsNone or IsSome instead.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.OptionPreferNoneOverDefault
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for suggesting Option.None instead of default(Option) or new Option().
member.remarks: This diagnostic promotes clearer intent by suggesting the explicit use of Option.None when creating an empty option value.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.OptionNoneMisuse
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting misuse of Option.None when assigned to non-generic Option variable.
member.remarks: This diagnostic warns when Option.None is assigned to a variable of type Option (non-generic) instead of being used directly or assigned to a properly typed Option<T> variable.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.UnhandledOption
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting Option values that are created but never consumed or checked.
member.remarks: This diagnostic helps prevent bugs where operations that return Option are called but their value is never examined, potentially ignoring important data.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.ResultValueAccessWithoutCheck
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting unsafe access to Result.Value without checking if the result is successful.
member.remarks: This diagnostic warns when code accesses the Value property of a Result without first verifying that IsSuccess is true, which could lead to an InvalidOperationException at runtime.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.ResultErrorAccessWithoutCheck
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting unsafe access to Result.Error without checking if the result has failed.
member.remarks: This diagnostic warns when code accesses the Error property of a Result without first verifying that IsFailure is true, which could lead to an InvalidOperationException at runtime.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.ResultPreferMatchOverIfElse
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for suggesting the use of Match method instead of if-else chains with Result types.
member.remarks: This diagnostic promotes more functional and idiomatic code by suggesting Match when developers use if (result.IsSuccess) else patterns.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.DiagnosticIds.UnhandledResult
member.summary: Diagnostic ID for detecting Result values that are created but never consumed or checked.
member.remarks: This diagnostic helps prevent bugs where operations that return Result are called but their success/failure status is never examined, potentially ignoring errors.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.SymbolHelpers
member.summary: Provides helper methods for identifying FluentUnions types in the Roslyn semantic model.
member.remarks: These utilities are used by analyzers to determine if a given type symbol represents one of the FluentUnions types (Option or Result). This is essential for ensuring analyzers only trigger on relevant code.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.SymbolHelpers.IsOptionType(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified type symbol represents an Option<T> type.
member.param: The type symbol to check.
member.returns: true if the type symbol represents Option<T>; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method checks that: - The type name is "Option" - It belongs to the "FluentUnions" namespace - It is a generic type with exactly one type argument

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.SymbolHelpers.IsResultType(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified type symbol represents any Result type (generic or non-generic).
member.param: The type symbol to check.
member.returns: true if the type symbol represents any Result type; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method checks if the type is named "Result" and belongs to the "FluentUnions" namespace, regardless of whether it's the generic Result<T> or non-generic Result type.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.SymbolHelpers.IsValueResultType(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified type symbol represents a generic Result<T> type with a value.
member.param: The type symbol to check.
member.returns: true if the type symbol represents Result<T>; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method specifically checks for the generic Result<T> type that can carry a value. Use this when you need to distinguish between Result<T> and the non-generic Result.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Common.SymbolHelpers.IsUnitResultType(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified type symbol represents a non-generic Result type (unit result).
member.param: The type symbol to check.
member.returns: true if the type symbol represents non-generic Result; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method checks for the non-generic Result type that represents operations that can succeed or fail but don't return a value on success.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider
member.summary: Provides code fixes for the OptionNoneMisuse diagnostic.
member.remarks:
This code fix provider offers two solutions for Option.None misuse: 1. Remove the variable declaration and use Option.None directly (when possible) 2. Change the variable type to Option<T> where T needs to be specified by the user
The first fix is preferred when the variable is only used once, while the second fix is offered when the variable needs to be retained for multiple uses.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.FixableDiagnosticIds
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic IDs that this provider can fix.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.GetFixAllProvider
member.summary: Gets the fix all provider for applying the fix to multiple diagnostics at once.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.RegisterCodeFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for the detected Option.None misuse diagnostics.
member.param: The code fix context containing the diagnostics and document.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.RegisterVariableDeclarationFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.VariableDeclaratorSyntax)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for variable declaration misuse.
member.param: The code fix context.
The variable declarator with Option.None assignment.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.RegisterAssignmentFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.AssignmentExpressionSyntax)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for assignment expression misuse.
member.param: The code fix context.
The assignment expression with Option.None.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.InlineOptionNoneAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Document,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.VariableDeclaratorSyntax,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Inlines Option.None by removing the variable and using Option.None directly at usage sites.
member.param: The document to modify.
The variable declarator to remove.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The modified document.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNoneMisuseCodeFixProvider.ChangeToGenericOptionAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Document,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.VariableDeclarationSyntax,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Changes the variable type from Option to Option<T> with a placeholder for T.
member.param: The document to modify.
The variable declaration to modify.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The modified document.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider
member.summary: Provides code fixes for the OptionNullComparison diagnostic by replacing null comparisons with appropriate Option property checks.
member.remarks:
This code fix provider handles two types of null comparison patterns: - Binary expressions (== null, != null): Replaced with IsNone or IsSome respectively - Is pattern expressions (is null): Replaced with IsNone
The code fix maintains the semantic meaning of the original comparison while using the proper Option API.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.FixableDiagnosticIds
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic IDs that this provider can fix.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.GetFixAllProvider
member.summary: Gets the fix all provider for applying the fix to multiple diagnostics at once.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.RegisterCodeFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for the detected null comparison diagnostics.
member.param: The code fix context containing the diagnostics and document.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.RegisterBinaryExpressionFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.BinaryExpressionSyntax)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for binary expression null comparisons (== null, != null).
member.param: The code fix context.
The binary expression comparing with null.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.RegisterIsPatternFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.IsPatternExpressionSyntax)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for 'is null' pattern expressions.
member.param: The code fix context.
The is pattern expression.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.ReplaceWithPropertyCheckAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Document,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.BinaryExpressionSyntax,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replaces a binary null comparison expression with an Option property check.
member.param: The document to modify.
The binary expression to replace.
The name of the Option property to use (IsNone or IsSome).
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The modified document.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.ReplaceIsPatternWithPropertyCheckAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Document,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.IsPatternExpressionSyntax,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replaces an 'is null' pattern expression with an Option property check.
member.param: The document to modify.
The is pattern expression to replace.
The name of the Option property to use (typically IsNone).
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The modified document.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionNullComparisonCodeFixProvider.IsNullLiteral(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified expression is a null literal.
member.param: The expression to check.
member.returns: true if the expression is a null literal; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionValueAccessCodeFixProvider
member.summary: Provides code fixes for the OptionValueAccessWithoutCheck diagnostic by adding proper safety checks.
member.remarks:
This code fix provider offers two approaches to fix unsafe Option.Value access: 1. Add IsSome check: Wraps the value access in an if statement that checks IsSome 2. Use Match method: Replaces the value access with a Match call that handles both Some and None cases
The Match approach is generally preferred as it forces handling of both cases explicitly, while the IsSome check is simpler for cases where None handling is not needed.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionValueAccessCodeFixProvider.FixableDiagnosticIds
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic IDs that this provider can fix.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionValueAccessCodeFixProvider.GetFixAllProvider
member.summary: Gets the fix all provider for applying the fix to multiple diagnostics at once.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionValueAccessCodeFixProvider.RegisterCodeFixesAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CodeFixes.CodeFixContext)
member.summary: Registers code fixes for unsafe Option.Value access diagnostics.
member.param: The code fix context containing the diagnostics and document.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionValueAccessCodeFixProvider.AddIsSomeCheckAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Document,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Adds an IsSome check around the unsafe value access.
member.param: The document to modify.
The member access expression accessing Value.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The modified document with the IsSome check added.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.CodeFixes.OptionValueAccessCodeFixProvider.UseMatchMethodAsync(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Document,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Replaces the unsafe value access with a Match method call.
member.param: The document to modify.
The member access expression accessing Value.
The cancellation token.
member.returns: The modified document with the Match method call.
member.remarks: The generated Match call includes a lambda for the Some case that contains the original expression, and a None case that throws an InvalidOperationException to maintain the original behavior.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects misuse of Option.None when assigned to a non-generic Option variable.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for the Option.None misuse rule.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic descriptors supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering analysis actions.
member.param: The analysis context used to register actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.AnalyzeVariableDeclaration(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes variable declarations for Option.None misuse.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.AnalyzeAssignment(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes assignment expressions for Option.None misuse.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.IsOptionNone(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified expression is Option.None.
member.param: The expression to check.
The semantic model.
member.returns: true if the expression is Option.None; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNoneMisuseAnalyzer.IsNonGenericOptionType(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified type is the non-generic Option type.
member.param: The type symbol to check.
member.returns: true if the type is non-generic Option; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects null comparisons with Option<T> types and suggests using IsNone or IsSome instead.
member.remarks:
Since Option<T> is a value type (struct), it can never be null. This analyzer detects common mistakes where developers treat Option like a reference type and compare it with null. The analyzer will trigger on: - Direct null comparisons: option == null, option != null - Pattern matching with null: option is null
The analyzer suggests using the appropriate Option properties: - Use IsNone to check if the option has no value - Use IsSome to check if the option has a value

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for the null comparison rule.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic descriptors supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering analysis actions for relevant syntax nodes.
member.param: The analysis context used to register actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer.AnalyzeBinaryExpression(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes binary expressions (== and !=) to detect null comparisons with Option types.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer.AnalyzeIsPattern(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes 'is' pattern expressions to detect null pattern matching with Option types.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionNullComparisonAnalyzer.IsNullLiteral(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax)
member.summary: Determines whether the specified expression is a null literal.
member.param: The expression to check.
member.returns: true if the expression is a null literal; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that suggests using the Match method instead of if/else patterns when handling Option types.
member.remarks:
This analyzer promotes a more functional programming style by detecting if/else statements that check IsSome or IsNone properties and suggesting the use of the Match method instead. The analyzer triggers when: - An if statement checks option.IsSome or option.IsNone (or their negations) - The if statement has an else clause - At least one branch accesses the Option value
The Match method provides better type safety and a more idiomatic approach to handling Option values, ensuring both Some and None cases are handled explicitly.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for the prefer Match rule.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic descriptors supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering analysis actions for if statements.
member.param: The analysis context used to register actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.AnalyzeIfStatement(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes if statements to detect patterns that could be replaced with Match method calls.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.GetOptionCheckInfo(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines if the condition expression is checking an Option's IsSome or IsNone property.
member.param: The condition expression to analyze.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: Information about the Option check if found; otherwise, null.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.BothBranchesHandleOption(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.IfStatementSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ElseClauseSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether both branches of an if/else statement access the Option value.
member.param: The if statement to analyze.
The else clause to analyze.
The Option expression being checked.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: true if at least one branch accesses the Option; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.IsAccessingOption(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether a member access expression is accessing the specified Option.
member.param: The member access expression to check.
The Option expression to compare against.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: true if the member access is accessing the specified Option; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.GetOptionTypeArgument(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Gets the type argument of an Option<T> type.
member.param: The expression of Option type.
The semantic model for type information.
member.returns: The type argument T from Option<T>; otherwise, null.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.OptionCheckInfo
member.summary: Contains information about an Option check in a condition.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.OptionCheckInfo.Expression
member.summary: Gets or sets the Option expression being checked.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferMatchAnalyzer.OptionCheckInfo.IsCheckingIsSome
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the check is for IsSome (true) or IsNone (false).

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that suggests using Option.None instead of default expressions when creating empty Option values.
member.remarks:
This analyzer promotes clarity and intent by detecting uses of default(Option<T>) or default literal expressions and suggesting the more expressive Option.None. The analyzer triggers on: - Explicit default expressions: default(Option<T>) - Default literal expressions: default when the target type is Option<T>
Using Option.None makes the code more readable and clearly expresses the intent to create an empty option value, rather than relying on the default value semantics.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for the prefer None rule.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic descriptors supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering analysis actions for default expressions.
member.param: The analysis context used to register actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer.AnalyzeDefaultExpression(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes explicit default expressions like default(Option<T>).
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer.AnalyzeDefaultLiteral(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes default literal expressions where the target type is Option<T>.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionPreferNoneAnalyzer.CheckAndReportIfOptionType(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Checks if the given type is an Option type and reports a diagnostic if it is.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.
The expression to report the diagnostic on.
The type to check.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects potentially unsafe access to Option.Value property without proper null checks.
member.remarks:
This analyzer helps prevent runtime InvalidOperationException by detecting when code accesses the Value property of an Option without first verifying that the option contains a value. The analyzer considers access safe when: - It's inside an if statement that checks IsSome - It's in the true branch of a conditional expression that checks IsSome - It's inside a Match method call - It's preceded by a logical AND condition that includes an IsSome check
This analyzer is crucial for maintaining runtime safety when working with Option types.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for the unsafe value access rule.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the list of diagnostic descriptors supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering analysis actions for member access expressions.
member.param: The analysis context used to register actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.AnalyzeMemberAccess(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes member access expressions to detect unsafe access to Option.Value.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.IsInSafeContext(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether the Value access occurs in a safe context where IsSome has been checked.
member.param: The Value property access expression.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: true if the access is in a safe context; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.HasIsSomeCheck(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Checks if the given condition expression contains an IsSome check for the specified option.
member.param: The condition expression to analyze.
The option expression to look for.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: true if the condition checks IsSome for the option; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.AreExpressionsEquivalent(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether two expressions refer to the same symbol.
member.param: The first expression.
The second expression.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: true if both expressions refer to the same symbol; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.OptionValueAccessAnalyzer.IsMatchMethodCall(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.InvocationExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether the invocation is a call to the Option.Match method.
member.param: The invocation expression to check.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: true if the invocation is an Option.Match call; otherwise, false.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects Option values that are created but never consumed or checked.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for unhandled Option values.
member.remarks: This rule produces warning FU0006 when an Option is created but not handled. This helps catch potential bugs where optional values are silently ignored.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the collection of diagnostics supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering actions to be performed during compilation.
member.param: The analysis context used to register analyzer actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.AnalyzeInvocation(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes method invocations to detect unhandled Option return values.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the invocation to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.AnalyzeObjectCreation(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes object creation expressions to detect unhandled Option instances.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the object creation to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.IsOptionHandled(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel,System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether an Option expression is being properly handled.
member.param: The Option expression to check.
The semantic model for analysis.
The name of the method that created the Option.
member.returns: True if the Option is being handled; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: An Option is considered handled if it is: - Assigned to a variable or field - Returned from a method - Passed as an argument to another method - Used with property access (IsSome, IsNone, Value) - Used with handling methods (Match, Map, Bind, GetValueOrThrow, etc.) - Used in pattern matching or conditional expressions

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.IsPureFunction(System.String)
member.summary: Determines if a method is a pure function (returns a value without side effects).
member.param: The name of the method to check.
member.returns: True if the method is a pure function; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.IsHandlingMethod(System.String)
member.summary: Determines if a method name represents an Option handling method.
member.param: The name of the method to check.
member.returns: True if the method handles Option; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Handling methods are those that consume or transform the Option in a meaningful way, ensuring that the presence/absence of values is properly addressed.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.GetSuggestionMessage(System.String)
member.summary: Gets an appropriate suggestion message based on the method.
member.param: The name of the method that created the Option.
member.returns: A suggestion message for handling the Option.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Option.UnhandledOptionAnalyzer.GetOptionTypeString(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Gets a display string for an Option type symbol.
member.param: The type symbol to convert to string.
member.returns: A string representation of the Option type (e.g., "Option<int>").

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects unsafe access to Result.Error property without proper failure checks.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for unsafe Result.Error access.
member.remarks: This rule produces warning FU0102 when Result.Error is accessed without proper checks. The warning can be suppressed if the developer is certain the access is safe.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the collection of diagnostics supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering actions to be performed during compilation.
member.param: The analysis context used to register analyzer actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.AnalyzeMemberAccess(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes member access expressions to detect unsafe Result.Error access.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the member access expression to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.IsInSafeContext(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether a Result.Error access is in a safe context where IsFailure has been checked.
member.param: The member access expression for Result.Error.
The semantic model for the compilation.
member.returns: True if the access is in a safe context; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method checks various patterns to determine safety: - If statements with IsFailure conditions - If statements with negated IsSuccess conditions - Conditional expressions with failure checks - Inside Match method failure callbacks (second argument)

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.HasIsFailureCheck(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Checks if an expression contains an IsFailure property check or negated IsSuccess check for the given result expression.
member.param: The condition expression to examine.
The result expression to match against.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if the condition checks failure state on the same result instance; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method recognizes both direct IsFailure checks and negated IsSuccess checks (!result.IsSuccess).

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.AreExpressionsEquivalent(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines if two expressions refer to the same symbol (variable, property, etc.).
member.param: The first expression to compare.
The second expression to compare.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if both expressions refer to the same symbol; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultErrorAccessAnalyzer.IsMatchMethodCall(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.InvocationExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Checks if an invocation is a call to the Match method on a Result type.
member.param: The invocation expression to check.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if the invocation is a Result.Match method call; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: In Match method calls, the Error property can be safely accessed within the failure handler (typically the second argument).

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that suggests using the Match method instead of if/else patterns for Result handling.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for suggesting Match over if/else patterns.
member.remarks: This rule produces info-level diagnostic FU0103 to suggest a more idiomatic approach. It's an informational suggestion rather than a warning, as if/else patterns are still valid.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the collection of diagnostics supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering actions to be performed during compilation.
member.param: The analysis context used to register analyzer actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.AnalyzeIfStatement(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes if statements to detect if/else patterns that could be replaced with Match.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the if statement to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.GetResultCheckInfo(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Extracts information about Result property checks from a condition expression.
member.param: The condition expression to analyze.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: Information about the Result check if found; otherwise, null.
member.remarks: This method handles both direct property access (result.IsSuccess, result.IsFailure) and negated forms (!result.IsSuccess, !result.IsFailure).

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.BothBranchesHandleResult(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.IfStatementSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ElseClauseSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines if both branches of an if/else statement handle the Result value.
member.param: The if statement to analyze.
The else clause to analyze.
The Result expression being checked.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if at least one branch accesses Result.Value or Result.Error; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.IsAccessingResult(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Checks if a member access expression is accessing Value or Error properties of a specific Result instance.
member.param: The member access expression to check.
The Result expression to match against.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if the member access is accessing Value or Error on the same Result instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.GetResultTypeString(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Gets a display string for the Result type of an expression.
member.param: The expression whose type to get.
The semantic model for type information.
member.returns: A string representation of the Result type (e.g., "Result<int>" or "Result").

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.ResultCheckInfo
member.summary: Holds information about a Result property check in a condition.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.ResultCheckInfo.Expression
member.summary: Gets or sets the expression that represents the Result instance being checked.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultPreferMatchAnalyzer.ResultCheckInfo.IsCheckingIsSuccess
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the check is for IsSuccess (true) or IsFailure (false).

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects unsafe access to Result.Value property without proper success checks.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for unsafe Result.Value access.
member.remarks: This rule produces warning FU0101 when Result.Value is accessed without proper checks. The warning can be suppressed if the developer is certain the access is safe.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the collection of diagnostics supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering actions to be performed during compilation.
member.param: The analysis context used to register analyzer actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.AnalyzeMemberAccess(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes member access expressions to detect unsafe Result.Value access.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the member access expression to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.IsInSafeContext(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines whether a Result.Value access is in a safe context where IsSuccess has been checked.
member.param: The member access expression for Result.Value.
The semantic model for the compilation.
member.returns: True if the access is in a safe context; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: This method checks various patterns to determine safety: - If statements with IsSuccess conditions - Conditional expressions with IsSuccess checks - Inside Match method callbacks - After ThrowIfFailure method calls

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.HasIsSuccessCheck(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Checks if an expression contains an IsSuccess property check for the given result expression.
member.param: The condition expression to examine.
The result expression to match against.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if the condition checks IsSuccess on the same result instance; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.IsAfterThrowIfFailureCall(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.MemberAccessExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines if a Result.Value access occurs after a ThrowIfFailure method call on the same result instance.
member.param: The member access expression for Result.Value.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if the access is after a ThrowIfFailure call; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: ThrowIfFailure ensures the result is successful or throws an exception, making subsequent Value access safe.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.AreExpressionsEquivalent(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Determines if two expressions refer to the same symbol (variable, property, etc.).
member.param: The first expression to compare.
The second expression to compare.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if both expressions refer to the same symbol; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.ResultValueAccessAnalyzer.IsMatchMethodCall(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.InvocationExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel)
member.summary: Checks if an invocation is a call to the Match method on a Result type.
member.param: The invocation expression to check.
The semantic model for symbol resolution.
member.returns: True if the invocation is a Result.Match method call; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Match method provides safe pattern matching on Result, making Value access within its callbacks safe.

member.name: T:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer
member.summary: Analyzer that detects Result values that are created but never consumed or checked.

member.name: F:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.Rule
member.summary: The diagnostic descriptor for unhandled Result values.
member.remarks: This rule produces warning FU0104 when a Result is created but not handled. This helps catch potential bugs where errors are silently ignored.

member.name: P:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.SupportedDiagnostics
member.summary: Gets the collection of diagnostics supported by this analyzer.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.Initialize(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.AnalysisContext)
member.summary: Initializes the analyzer by registering actions to be performed during compilation.
member.param: The analysis context used to register analyzer actions.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.AnalyzeInvocation(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes method invocations to detect unhandled Result return values.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the invocation to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.AnalyzeObjectCreation(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Diagnostics.SyntaxNodeAnalysisContext)
member.summary: Analyzes object creation expressions to detect unhandled Result instances.
member.param: The syntax node analysis context containing the object creation to analyze.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.IsResultHandled(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Syntax.ExpressionSyntax,Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.SemanticModel,System.String)
member.summary: Determines whether a Result expression is being properly handled.
member.param: The Result expression to check.
The semantic model for analysis.
The name of the method that created the Result.
member.returns: True if the Result is being handled; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: A Result is considered handled if it is: - Assigned to a variable or field - Returned from a method - Passed as an argument to another method - Used with property access (IsSuccess, IsFailure, Value, Error) - Used with handling methods (Match, Map, Bind, ThrowIfFailure, etc.) - Used in pattern matching or conditional expressions

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.IsPureFunction(System.String)
member.summary: Determines if a method is a pure function (returns a value without side effects).
member.param: The name of the method to check.
member.returns: True if the method is a pure function; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.IsHandlingMethod(System.String)
member.summary: Determines if a method name represents a Result handling method.
member.param: The name of the method to check.
member.returns: True if the method handles Result; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Handling methods are those that consume or transform the Result in a meaningful way, ensuring that success/failure states are properly addressed.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.GetSuggestionMessage(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol,System.String)
member.summary: Gets an appropriate suggestion message based on the Result type and method.
member.param: The Result type.
The name of the method that created the Result.
member.returns: A suggestion message for handling the Result.

member.name: M:FluentUnions.Analyzers.Result.UnhandledResultAnalyzer.GetResultTypeString(Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.ITypeSymbol)
member.summary: Gets a display string for a Result type symbol.
member.param: The type symbol to convert to string.
member.returns: A string representation of the Result type (e.g., "Result<int>" or "Result").
